results.possibleIdentity: Howard MacPhee was an electrical engineer active in the Philadelphia area during the late 1960s and 1970s. He earned a Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering from the Drexel Institute of Technology (now Drexel University) in 1966. He was employed by Philco-Ford, a major aerospace and electronics company based in the Philadelphia region. MacPhee was a technical participant in Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization that facilitated collaborations between artists and engineers. His technical profile was exceptionally broad, encompassing fields such as aerospace, computers, acoustics, audio-visual conversions, communications, and digital systems. His specialized knowledge included strobes, tape recording, instrumentation and control systems, remote control, motors, circuit design, integrated circuits, transistors, microwaves, and environmental simulation.
